tasks.description: Add search API config to config.py: GOOGLE_API_KEY and GOOGLE_CX from env vars, SOURCE_LABS list (20 labs: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta FAIR, xAI, Mistral, Cohere, AI21 Labs, Inflection AI, Stability AI, Character.ai, Adept, Amazon AGI, Apple ML, Microsoft Research AI, NVIDIA Research, Baidu AI, ByteDance AI, Samsung AI, Alibaba DAMO — each with name and query variants), SEARCH_QUERY_TEMPLATES list (site:linkedin.com/in patterns), SEARCH_RATE_LIMIT_DELAY=1.5. Then create search_client.py: GoogleSearchClient class wrapping Google Custom Search JSON API (free tier 100/day). Methods: search(query, num=10) returns list of {title, url, snippet}. Handle rate limiting, 429 errors, empty responses. Add tests/test_search_client.py with mocked requests.

Add talent_moves and company_discovery tables to db.py. talent_moves: id, person_name, linkedin_url (UNIQUE), previous_lab, previous_title, current_company, current_title, discovered_at, source_query. company_discovery: id, company_name (UNIQUE), talent_count, talent_sources (JSON), category, funding, founded, hq_location, careers_url, website, description, first_seen, enriched (bool), added_to_pipeline (bool). Add indexes on previous_lab, current_company, talent_count DESC. Add helper functions: insert_talent_move (INSERT OR IGNORE by linkedin_url), insert_discovered_company (INSERT OR REPLACE), get_talent_moves_by_lab, get_top_companies_by_talent. Call _init_talent_tables from init_db. Add tests/test_db_talent.py.

Create profile_parser.py: parse_search_result(result, source_lab) function. Takes a Google search result dict {title, url, snippet} and extracts: person_name, linkedin_url, current_company, current_title, previous_lab, previous_title. Handle LinkedIn title formats: 'Name - Title at Company | LinkedIn', 'Name - Title, Company | LinkedIn', 'Name - Title @ Company'. Strip '| LinkedIn' suffix. Return None for unparseable results or non-profile URLs (must contain /in/). Add tests/test_profile_parser.py with 5+ test cases covering all formats.

Create talent_discovery.py: TalentDiscovery class. __init__ takes sqlite3 connection. discover_lab(lab_name, queries, max_queries) runs SEARCH_QUERY_TEMPLATES for each query variant, calls GoogleSearchClient.search(), parses results via profile_parser.parse_search_result(), skips if current_company matches source lab (still there), inserts into talent_moves via db. discover_all(max_queries_per_lab) iterates SOURCE_LABS. Track stats: queries run, profiles found, profiles stored. Add tests/test_talent_discovery.py with mocked search results.

Create company_aggregator.py: aggregate_companies(conn, min_talent=2) function. Query talent_moves grouped by current_company and previous_lab. Build company_map: {company: {lab: count}}. For each company with total >= min_talent, insert into company_discovery table. Return list of inserted companies. Add tests/test_company_aggregator.py verifying correct counts, source tracking, and min_talent filtering.

Create company_enricher.py: enrich_company(conn, company_name) searches Google for '{company} AI startup funding' and extracts: category (using keyword matching for foundation-model/robotics/ai-infra/ai-app/ai-agent/ai-safety/ai-chip), funding (regex for dollar amounts), hq_location (match against known cities), website, description. Updates company_discovery row, sets enriched=1. enrich_all_companies(conn) processes all un-enriched companies. Skip already enriched. Add tests/test_company_enricher.py.

Create tracker.py: generate_tracker_md(conn, output_path) generates a markdown file with: (1) ranked table of companies by talent signal (rank, name, talent inflow with per-lab breakdown, category, funding, HQ), (2) stealth section for companies with no website/funding. Write to output_path if provided. Add tests/test_tracker.py verifying markdown contains correct companies in rank order.

Create talent_charts.py with 3 functions: (1) generate_sankey(conn, output_path) — Plotly Sankey diagram showing flow from source labs to destination companies, save as HTML. (2) generate_company_ranking_bar(conn, output_path) — matplotlib horizontal bar chart of companies by talent count, save as PNG. (3) generate_talent_heatmap(conn, output_path) — matplotlib heatmap of source lab x destination company with annotated counts, save as PNG. Add tests/test_talent_charts.py verifying files are created.

Add iteration 2 subcommands to main.py: 'discover' (runs TalentDiscovery.discover_all + aggregate_companies, accepts --max-queries-per-lab and --min-talent), 'enrich' (runs enrich_all_companies), 'track' (generates tracker.md + all 3 talent charts to output/), 'discover-all' (runs discover + enrich + track in sequence). Add tests/test_main_iter2.py verifying --help works for each subcommand.

Create pipeline.py: get_companies_for_crawling(conn) returns discovered companies with careers_url set and added_to_pipeline=0. mark_company_crawled(conn, name) sets added_to_pipeline=1. run_full_pipeline(conn) gets companies, runs JobCrawler on each, marks crawled, regenerates tracker and charts. Add tests/test_pipeline.py.

End-to-end smoke test: create tests/test_e2e_iter2.py. Mock GoogleSearchClient to return 5 fake LinkedIn search results across 2 labs. Run full pipeline: discover -> aggregate -> enrich (mocked) -> generate tracker.md. Verify: talent_moves has records, company_discovery has aggregated companies, tracker.md contains company names ranked correctly. All with in-memory SQLite.

Create docs/setup-google-search.md with step-by-step instructions: (1) create Google Cloud project, enable Custom Search JSON API, (2) get API key, (3) create Programmable Search Engine scoped to linkedin.com/in/*, get CX ID, (4) set GOOGLE_API_KEY and GOOGLE_CX env vars (bash and PowerShell), (5) free tier limits (100/day), (6) test command. Commit all iteration 2 files.
